Generally, a partnership doesn't pay tax on its income but passes through any profits or losses to its partners.
Divya's income from the partnership is $8,000, and the remaining $12,000 of partnership income will be reported by the other partners in proportion to their shares under the partnership agreement.
However, because the partnership is allowed only $400 per year of depreciation (10% of $4,000), no more than $400 can be allocated between the partners.
All the partners must agree to make the choice, and the partners must be able to figure their own taxable income without figuring the partnership's income.
